content: Just finished moving into Linford Hall and I'm already exhausted. My roommate Kwame seems cool though, he helped me carry like three boxes up four flights of stairs because the elevator was broken. He's from Connecticut and really into photography apparently, has this whole setup with lights and backdrops stuffed in his closet.
The dining hall food is questionable at best. Had something they called 'chicken parmesan' but I'm not convinced it was chicken. Might have been a social experiment.

Met the RA during the floor meeting, her name's Mira. She went through all the rules about quiet hours and guests and whatever. Seemed pretty strict about it honestly, kept emphasizing the noise policy like she'd had problems before.
why is the wifi so bad
Kwame's already blasting music and it's the first night. This is going to be interesting. He's got decent taste though, lot of Afrobeats and some older hip hop.

First day of classes tomorrow and I still haven't figured out where half the buildings are. Westbrook's campus is so spread out compared to my old school, like why is the science building a fifteen minute walk from everything else. Makes zero sense from a planning perspective.
Organic chemistry is going to destroy me. Professor Huang seems brilliant but also terrifying? She has this way of looking at you like she knows you didn't do the reading. Which I didn't. But still.

There's a guy in my orgo class, Nico, who showed up twenty minutes late and just walked right to the front row like it was nothing. Professor Huang didn't even acknowledge him which somehow made it worse. Respect the confidence though.
found a coffee shop on campus called The Grind and it's actually good?? the iced vanilla latte saved my life after that three hour lab
Do you think pigeons have complex inner lives? There's one outside my window that just stares. Every day. Same spot. What does it want from me.

Psych 201 seems manageable. Got paired with this girl Layla for the semester project, she seems organized which is good because I am absolutely not. We're doing something about sleep deprivation and memory, ironic given I slept maybe four hours last night.

The guy across the hall, Tobias, knocked on our door to ask if we had a phone charger. Ended up talking for like an hour about random stuff. He's loud but funny, does this thing where he narrates everything like a sports commentator.
His girlfriend Celeste was with him and she's really nice actually, she's an art major and showed me some of her portfolio on her phone. Really into like surrealist stuff, melting clocks vibes.
I should probably join some clubs or something. Saw a flyer for intramural volleyball, might check it out since I played a bit in high school. Nothing serious but it could be fun.
rain

It's been raining for two days straight and I forgot to bring an umbrella to this school like an idiot. Had to sprint from the science building to the library and now my notes are soggy. This is fine.

Started a study group for orgo with a few people from class. Beatrice is pre-med and takes incredibly color-coded notes, Nico is perpetually confused but asks good questions, and there's this quiet guy Dmitri who doesn't say much but somehow always knows the answers.

Beatrice mentioned her sister is also pre-med at some school in California. Apparently their parents are both doctors so the pressure is intense. Glad my family doesn't have those kinds of expectations honestly.
why do i keep buying energy drinks knowing they make me feel terrible. this is a cycle i cannot escape. the blue one is so good though.

Went to the volleyball tryouts and it was actually really fun. The team captain Hana is super welcoming, she's been playing since she was like ten and you can tell. Made everyone feel included even though some of us were clearly rusty.

Met some of the other players too. Reggie plays middle blocker and is absurdly tall, like he has to duck through doorways tall. Sienna is the setter and she's got this intense focus during drills but was really chill afterwards.
Coach Tran watched from the sidelines taking notes the whole time. Didn't say much but apparently that's normal, Hana said he only really talks during actual games and practices.
I made the team!! Intramural so it's not super competitive but still feels good. Practice twice a week, games on weekends. Finally have something structured to do besides studying.
Called mom to tell her about volleyball and she went on a thirty minute tangent about her garden. Apparently the tomatoes are doing well but something is eating the basil. Riveting stuff.

Grandma Lucia called right after, her Sunday timing is impeccable. She asked if I was eating enough approximately seven times and told me about her neighbor's cat who learned to open doors. I love her.
Tobias had people over last night and it was loud until like 2am. Kwame was annoyed but didn't say anything. The walls here are paper thin, I could literally hear them playing Mario Kart.
The Grind is hiring and I kind of need money so I might apply? Working at a coffee shop seems manageable and I'm there all the time anyway might as well get paid for it.

Submitted my application. The manager Oscar was there and seemed nice, asked me a few questions on the spot about availability and whether I'd had food service experience. I said I worked at a smoothie place in high school which is technically true even though I only lasted two months.

Professor Huang's TA held office hours today. Ronan is a grad student who looks perpetually exhausted but explained reaction mechanisms way better than the textbook. Might actually survive this class.

There's a girl at the end of our hall, Yuki, who I've seen maybe three times total. She just kind of appears and disappears. Kwame says she's a music composition major and basically lives in the practice rooms.
Do birds sleep? Like actually sleep? I've never seen a sleeping bird. Where do they go at night. This is bothering me more than it should.
got the job at The Grind!! start training next week. Oscar said he liked my 'energy' which I think means I seemed desperate enough to show up on time.
Kwame is pumped for me, says now I can hook him up with free coffee. I don't think that's how it works but I'll figure something out. He's been a good roommate so far, we've found a rhythm.

First volleyball practice was tough. My arms are going to be so bruised from all the bumping drills. Hana pushed everyone hard but it felt productive. Sienna gave me tips on positioning that actually helped.

Reggie invited everyone to get food after practice and we ended up at this place called Sunrise Diner off campus. The pancakes were enormous and cheap, might become a regular spot. The decor was aggressively 1970s, lots of orange and brown.

Started training at The Grind today. Oscar showed me the basics - register, espresso machine, how to deal with the morning rush. He's patient but clearly has done this a million times, got that efficient manager energy.

One of my coworkers is a senior named Frankie who's been there forever apparently. He knows all the regulars by name and order, it's impressive. Oscar kept having him demonstrate stuff because he's faster on the espresso machine than anyone.

Oscar spent like twenty minutes teaching Frankie some new technique for the latte art, they were both super focused on getting the leaf pattern right. Frankie was joking that Oscar's a perfectionist but you could tell he appreciated learning it.
The other new hire is Juno, also a student. She's a sophomore and seems a little overwhelmed but same honestly. We bonded over not knowing where anything is stored.
why is there a squirrel in the quad that has no fear. it walked up to me and just stared. waited. i gave it part of my granola bar because i panicked. i have been chosen.

Study group met at the library today. Beatrice brought homemade flashcards for everyone which was incredibly nice but also made me feel like an underachiever. Nico showed up thirty minutes late with no excuse, just vibes.

Dmitri finally talked more today, turns out he's from Belarus and has been in the US for like three years. His English is perfect but he said he's self-conscious about his accent which is why he's quiet. Couldn't even tell he had one.
There's this old guy who plays chess in the quad every afternoon. Just sits there with a board waiting for opponents. I watched him destroy three different people today. King behavior honestly.

Volleyball game tomorrow against Eastwood Hall. Nervous but excited. Hana gave this whole speech about teamwork and trusting the process which was either inspiring or cheesy depending on your perspective.

Went to the gym before the game to warm up and ran into Tobias of all people. Didn't know he worked out but apparently he's trying to 'get in shape' for unspecified reasons. He was doing bicep curls wrong and I didn't have the heart to tell him.

WE WON!! It was close but Sienna's sets were perfect and Hana dominated at the net. I played decent, didn't embarrass myself at least. The team chemistry is already better than I expected for a group that just met.

Post-game celebration at Theo's house. Didn't know who Theo was before today but apparently he throws parties constantly and just invites everyone. Off-campus house that's seen better days but the vibes were good.

Met Zara at the party and we talked for like two hours about nothing and everything. She's a sociology major with strong opinions about capitalism and also knows way too much about reality TV. Interesting combo.
She was there with this guy Malik who's apparently her... something? They kept giving each other looks. The situationship energy was palpable. None of my business but I'm invested now.
hungover

Kwame made me drink water and eat toast like I was a sick child. Good roommate. He didn't go out last night, stayed in editing photos. He's working on some series about urban architecture, showed me a few shots and they're actually really good.

Mira knocked on the door to ask if everything was okay because apparently I was loud coming back last night. Mortifying. She wasn't mean about it but I could feel the judgment. Kwame just hid his speaker under his bed preemptively.

Why is the library always either completely empty or packed with nowhere to sit? There is no in between. Today it was packed and I had to sit next to some guy who was eating chips LOUDLY. In the quiet section. Unhinged behavior.
Layla and I worked on our psych project outline. She's already got a whole bibliography started and we haven't even decided our hypothesis yet. Type A energy but I'm grateful someone is on top of it.
First shift at The Grind without Oscar hovering. Frankie was in charge and he's way more relaxed about stuff. Let me practice the espresso machine during slow periods instead of just cleaning.

There's this regular, an older professor type, who comes in every day at exactly 2pm and orders the same thing. Black coffee, no room. Frankie knows his name but I forgot it immediately. Something with a J maybe.
Juno had a mini breakdown in the back because she messed up an order and the customer was rude about it. We hid and ate scones together until she felt better. Solidarity.

I think I'm getting the hang of this job actually. The morning rush is chaos but there's something satisfying about the rhythm of it once you figure out the flow. Oscar noticed I was faster today and nodded approvingly which felt like winning an award.
How do people read in moving vehicles without getting sick? I tried to review notes on the bus and nearly threw up. This seems like an evolutionary disadvantage that should have been fixed by now.

Professor Huang called on me in class and I somehow knew the answer?? The look of mild surprise on her face was validation enough to carry me through the week. Beatrice high-fived me after which was sweet.

Ronan held extra review sessions for the exam and like four people showed up. His explanations are so clear, honestly better than the lectures. He seems tired though, mentioned he's working on his thesis and TAing two classes.

Saw Zara in the student center and she waved me over. We got smoothies and she filled me in on the Malik drama. Apparently they've been on and off for months and she doesn't know what she wants. Classic.
She mentioned her friend Ivy might join our hangouts soon. Ivy's apparently Beatrice's friend too which is a weird coincidence? Small campus I guess.

The weather finally cooled down and campus is actually pretty in fall. All the trees turning colors, people sitting on the quad studying. Very picturesque. Very 'I made the right choice transferring here' energy.

Called Cameron from back home and we talked for an hour catching up. He's at state school and apparently already failing calculus which tracks. Same old Cameron. He might visit in spring which would be cool.
What if clouds are just sky pillows. What if the sky is tired.

Volleyball practice was brutal today. Coach Tran actually spoke for once and it was all criticism. Constructive but still. My serve is apparently 'technically acceptable but lacks conviction' which is oddly personal.

Sienna stayed after to help me with serving technique. She's really good at breaking down the mechanics. Mentioned she used to play club volleyball competitively before deciding she wanted a life outside of sports.
First orgo exam tomorrow and I'm oscillating between feeling prepared and feeling like I know nothing. There is no in between. Beatrice made a study guide that's seventeen pages long. Seventeen.
Kwame wished me luck and offered to quiz me on structures. He's a communications major and understood nothing but his enthusiasm was helpful. He mispronounced 'benzene' in three different ways.
Couldn't sleep so I'm just staring at molecular models hoping osmosis is real. Outside my window the pigeon is back. We're both up at 3am. We understand each other.

Exam went... okay? Hard to tell with Huang. She walked around the room the entire time which was deeply stressful. Saw Nico finish in like thirty minutes and leave which either means he aced it or gave up entirely.

Mira was doing room checks tonight and Kwame frantically hid his speaker again even though it wasn't even on. His paranoia about getting written up is intense. He said he can't have anything on his record because of some scholarship thing.

The maintenance guy came to fix our radiator which apparently has been broken since before we moved in. He was chatty, told me about his daughter's wedding and how expensive flowers are. Random but pleasant.

Frankie was in a weird mood at work today. Kept messing up orders which never happens. Didn't want to pry but something's definitely going on. He left early and Oscar covered the rest of his shift without asking questions.
Juno thinks Frankie's going through a breakup. She heard him on the phone in the back sounding upset. I didn't hear anything but Juno has freakishly good hearing apparently.

Met with Dr. Adebayo, my advisor, to talk about next semester's schedule. She's really encouraging but also gently suggested I 'reconsider my time management strategies' which is advisor-speak for 'get your act together.'

Started watching this show about a detective who's also a pastry chef. It makes no sense and I can't stop watching. Kwame got sucked in too and now we have a standing Tuesday night viewing party of two.
The dining hall had actually good pasta today?? I'm suspicious. This feels like a trap. They're lulling us into a false sense of security before hitting us with mystery meat again.

Zara, Malik, and I hung out at Theo's place. It was awkward at first because I could feel the tension between them but then we played Mario Kart and vibes improved. I won twice which Malik was salty about.

Got my orgo exam back. B+!! Huang wrote 'good improvement' which from her is basically a standing ovation. Beatrice got an A obviously. Nico got a C- and seemed genuinely shocked which suggests he did not in fact give up.

Dmitri got the highest score in the class and seemed embarrassed about it. He's definitely one of those people who's brilliant but doesn't want attention. Beatrice immediately tried to recruit him as a permanent study group member.
i need to do laundry desperately but i also don't want to. what if i just bought new clothes. is that irresponsible. probably. going to do laundry.

The laundry room was packed and I had to wait for a machine. Made small talk with some girl from another floor who was also waiting. She told me about her major (marine biology) and her fish tank (named all the fish after philosophers). Never got her name though.
Volleyball game against Crawford House tomorrow. They're apparently pretty good so Hana's been sending motivational texts to the group chat all day. Reggie responded with nothing but fire emojis.

Couldn't sleep again so I walked around campus at like 1am. It's weirdly peaceful when no one's around. Found a bench by the fountain and just sat there for a while. Probably looked like a creep but whatever.

We lost to Crawford but it was close. Their setter was insanely good and kept catching us off guard. Sienna was frustrated after but handled it well, already talking about what we need to work on. That's the mentality I guess.
Some guy at the game kept yelling weird stuff from the sidelines and eventually got told to leave. Apparently he was from Crawford and had been drinking. College sports are wild.

Went to Sunrise Diner with the team after to drown our sorrows in pancakes. Hana gave a speech about learning from losses and not dwelling. She's a natural leader honestly, makes you want to try harder.
October already?? Time is fake and I refuse to acknowledge it. Still feels like I just moved in but apparently I've been here a month. Wild.

Frankie seems better at work. Still a little off but functional. Oscar asked him privately if everything was okay and Frankie just said 'relationship stuff' and Oscar nodded like that explained everything. Maybe it does.

There's a guy who comes into The Grind every day and orders the most complicated drink I've ever heard. Oat milk latte with two pumps vanilla one pump hazelnut extra hot but not too hot light foam. I've started preparing it when I see him walk in. He tips well though.

Psych project is coming along. Layla and I ran a pilot survey and got some interesting preliminary data. Our hypothesis about sleep deprivation affecting short-term memory recall seems to be holding up which is exciting.
Professor Klein apparently assigns like 200 pages of reading a week. My friend back home who has her is suffering. So glad I didn't take that class even though the topic sounded interesting.
Zara texted asking if I wanted to get food with her and Ivy tomorrow. Finally meeting the mysterious Ivy. Zara described her as 'intense but in a good way' which could mean anything.
can't find my student ID. searched everywhere. it's gone. this is a disaster. how do I get food. how do I exist.
Found it in my jeans from three days ago. The relief was physical. Kwame watched me panic for an hour before casually suggesting I check pockets. Could have mentioned that earlier.

Met Ivy for lunch with Zara. She IS intense. Like, she asked me three questions about my major and future plans within the first five minutes. But she's also funny and clearly cares a lot about her friends. I get it.

Ivy mentioned she knows Beatrice from some pre-med society thing. Apparently they did a volunteer event together last year. It's wild how connected everyone is on this campus even if they don't hang out directly.
The weather got cold overnight and I was not prepared. Had to layer three shirts to walk to class. Need to dig out my actual jacket from whatever box it's still buried in.
Brandon from high school posted on Instagram that he's dropping out to become a DJ. His parents must be thrilled. We weren't close but I remember him being pretty smart, weird pivot.

Study group was productive today. Beatrice had a color-coding crisis because she ran out of her specific shade of pink highlighter and none of the alternatives were 'right.' We found it eventually in her bag's secret pocket.
Nico brought snacks which partially redeems his chronic lateness. He makes these cookies that are dangerously good. Wouldn't share the recipe though, said it's a family secret. Dramatic.

There's this weird guy at the bus stop near campus who talks to himself. Not in a concerning way, more like he's practicing speeches? Saw him again today rehearsing what sounded like a TED talk about soil. Niche content.

Tobias invited Kwame and me to watch a movie in his room. Celeste was there painting her nails while we watched. It was some action movie I'd already seen but the company was nice. She's got a really dry sense of humor I didn't notice before.
What's the point of pennies. They cost more to make than they're worth. We as a society should just agree to round everything. This isn't a hot take this is basic logic.

Work was slow today so Oscar let us experiment with new drink recipes. I made something with lavender and honey that was actually decent. Frankie made something he called 'The Punisher' which was just espresso on espresso. Juno wisely abstained.
One of the regulars, this older woman named Margaret, told me I remind her of her grandson. I don't know if that's a compliment or not but she tipped five dollars so I'll take it.

Hana called an extra practice for tomorrow because she watched film of our last game and 'identified key areas for improvement.' She sent a literal PowerPoint to the group chat. This woman is committed.

Can't stop thinking about those spicy peanut noodles from that place on 4th street. Went there twice this week already. The owner recognizes me now which is either a good sign or means I need to diversify my diet.

Extra practice was intense. Hana wasn't wrong about the areas for improvement. My blocking timing is off and I know it. Reggie gave me some pointers that helped, he's got good instincts for positioning.
Sienna was on fire today. Her sets were crisp, timing perfect. After practice she seemed distracted though, kept checking her phone. Didn't ask about it, not my business.

Grandma Lucia called and spent twenty minutes describing a casserole she made. In vivid detail. Like I could taste it through the phone. She's going to mail me cookies apparently, says I sound 'too thin' which doesn't make sense as a sound but okay.

Library was quiet for once and I actually got a lot of work done. Found a corner spot on the third floor that might be my new secret study location. No one there except this one guy who was asleep with his textbook as a pillow.
Kwame's been working on a big photography project for class. He's doing portraits of people in their 'natural environments' and asked if he could photograph me at The Grind. Told him I'd ask Oscar.
Professor Huang announced that the next unit is on stereochemistry and that 'many students find this challenging.' The way she said it was ominous. Beatrice immediately started making new flashcards.

There's a cat that lives near the science building. No collar, probably feral, but extremely friendly. It walked with me to class like a little escort. We've bonded. I've named it Professor Whiskers in my head.

My cousin's roommate apparently went viral on TikTok for something to do with cheese? The full story is confusing but my cousin keeps sending me the video and I still don't get it. Gen Z humor I guess. Am I old.
Zara's officially done with Malik. She texted at midnight saying they had 'the talk' and it's over. Not sure how to respond to these situations so I just sent a heart emoji and offered ice cream.

Emergency ice cream hangout with Zara. She's sad but also relieved? Says she knew it wasn't going anywhere but breaking the pattern was hard. We watched trash reality TV and she ate an entire pint of cookie dough. Healing.
Do fish get thirsty. They're surrounded by water but can they drink it. This is keeping me up. I should study but instead I'm thinking about fish hydration.

Oscar approved Kwame photographing me at The Grind. He's actually kind of into it, said it could be good for the shop's social media. Frankie made a joke about being 'camera ready' and fixed his hair dramatically.
Kwame came during my shift and took like two hundred photos. It was weird being observed while making lattes but he promised to show me the good ones. Juno photobombed several on purpose.
The chess guy in the quad is there rain or shine apparently. Saw him today with an umbrella clamped to his chair, just playing like nothing was weird. Dedication to the craft.

Psych presentation went well. Layla did most of the talking because she's better at public speaking but I handled the Q&A. Professor seemed impressed with our methodology. One step closer to surviving this semester.

Bumped into Dmitri at the student center and we ended up getting coffee together. He's actually really interesting once he opens up. Grew up in Minsk, moved here for his mom's job, wants to go into research chemistry. Smart dude.
He mentioned he plays chess competitively which tracks. Might introduce him to quad chess guy. Either they'd become best friends or mortal enemies.
Why are textbooks so expensive. I just need one chapter. ONE CHAPTER. And they want $200. This is a scam. Found a PDF online that's probably illegal but I'm too broke to care.

Volleyball game against Monroe House and we CRUSHED them. Like it wasn't even close. Hana's extra practices paid off. Everyone's timing was on point. Sienna had the game of her life, every set was perfect.

Celebrated at Theo's again. He's basically the unofficial team sponsor at this point. His house is always available and he always has snacks. I asked what his major was and he said 'undeclared for life' which is either a joke or a philosophy.
Met some of Theo's housemates. All equally chill, all equally vague about their academic situations. One of them, DJ something, was setting up speakers in the corner. Never caught his full name.
Malik was there, which was awkward because Zara was too. They nodded at each other and then stayed on opposite sides of the room all night. Very mature. Very obvious.
Slept until 2pm and regret nothing. Sometimes your body just needs to recover. Kwame was gone all day doing photography stuff for his project. The room was peacefully quiet.
Mira sent a floor-wide email about keeping the common areas clean. It was passive aggressive in the most RA way possible. 'Gentle reminder' is doing a lot of work in that sentence.
The dining hall had a 'cultural food night' which was ambitious but the execution was questionable. The sushi was just rice with something pink on top. I appreciate the effort though.

Orgo is kicking my butt. Stereochemistry is exactly as bad as Huang warned. Looking at molecules and trying to determine if they're mirror images is making my brain hurt. Beatrice is somehow thriving.

Ronan held office hours and I stayed the entire time. He has this way of explaining chirality that actually makes sense. I took three pages of notes just from his examples. Grad students who are good at teaching are a gift.

Some girl in my psych lecture has been sitting next to me for weeks and we've never spoken. We just nod at each other. It's a weird non-relationship that I don't know how to change at this point. Too late to introduce myself.
Frankie was in a good mood at work today. Laughing, making jokes with customers. Big change from a few weeks ago. Didn't ask what changed but it's nice to see. Oscar noticed too and seemed relieved.

Juno and I were talking during a slow period and she mentioned she's thinking about transferring. Doesn't like the vibe here, misses her friends back home. That's sad, she's cool. Hope she figures it out.
What if we lived in a world where everyone's thoughts were visible above their heads. Society would collapse immediately. No one could handle that level of honesty.

Called home and dad answered for once instead of mom. We talked about football for twenty minutes which is more than we usually talk about total. Progress I guess. He asked if I was 'doing okay up there' which is dad-speak for caring.

Zara seems better about the Malik thing. She's thrown herself into her sociology thesis proposal and talks about inequality statistics with genuine passion now. Processing through academics, I respect it.
The squirrel in the quad has recruited allies. There are now three of them that approach people for food. They're organized. This is concerning.

Study group met at Beatrice's apartment off campus for once. She has her own place which is wild, turns out her family helps with rent. Nice space though, way better than the library. Nico brought his mystery cookies again.
Beatrice has a cat named Molecule which is extremely on-brand for a pre-med student. It sat on my notes the entire time and wouldn't move. Molecule has no respect for organic chemistry.

Walked back to campus with Dmitri and we talked about life stuff. He's homesick sometimes but says it's better here than he expected. His mom works at some tech company downtown, travels a lot. He's used to being independent.
The pigeon outside my window has a friend now. Two pigeons. Both staring. The council grows.

Volleyball practice and we drilled serves for like an hour straight. My shoulder is going to be sore tomorrow but my accuracy improved. Coach Tran gave me a nod which is basically a standing ovation from him.

Hana organized a team dinner at the Thai place near campus. Everyone came, which was nice. Reggie told stories about his high school team that were probably exaggerated but entertaining. Sienna was quiet, scrolling through her phone between conversations.
rain again

Spent the rainy Sunday doing absolutely nothing productive. Watched four episodes of the pastry detective show. Kwame joined for the last two. We're emotionally invested in whether Chef Inspector Moreau catches the croissant thief.
Mira knocked on our door to remind us about quiet hours because apparently someone on the floor complained about noise. Kwame swears it wasn't us but I did yell at the TV during a plot twist. Oops.
Yuki from down the hall actually spoke to me in the hallway. She asked if I was the one who yelled 'IT WAS THE BAKER ALL ALONG' at 10pm. I confirmed. She said 'mood' and kept walking. Progress.

Oscar was stressed at work today because the espresso machine was acting up. Frankie tried to fix it and somehow made it worse. Had to close early for repairs. Customers were understanding but Oscar looked like he aged five years.

Got sent home early so I wandered around campus. Ended up in the art building looking at student exhibitions. Some really cool stuff, some really pretentious stuff. One piece was literally a blank canvas titled 'Everything and Nothing.' Okay.

Celeste's work was actually displayed there, recognized her style from what she showed me on her phone. Hers was a painting of a door slightly open with light coming through. Simple but effective. Sent her a text saying I saw it.

Midterms approaching and the library is suddenly packed at all hours. Lost my secret spot on the third floor to someone who's apparently living there now based on the blanket and pillow situation. Respect the commitment.
Layla and I finished our psych paper draft. Just needs editing now. She's a solid partner, does her share, communicates clearly. Group projects usually suck but this one's been fine.

I think I understand stereochemistry now? Like actually understand it, not just memorized patterns. Ronan's office hours plus Beatrice's flashcards plus sheer desperation equals comprehension apparently.

Tobias is going through something. Heard him and Celeste arguing through the wall, couldn't make out words but the tone was heated. Kwame and I pretended we didn't hear anything. Thin walls are a curse.

The maintenance guy came back to check on the radiator and remembered the whole conversation about his daughter's wedding. It was apparently last weekend and he showed me pictures. Very wholesome. Radiator works perfectly now.
Grandma's cookies arrived in the mail. She sent enough for like twenty people so Kwame and I have been eating them with every meal. Snickerdoodles hit different when they're made with grandma love.
Shared some cookies at work and now everyone loves Grandma Lucia. Frankie ate like six. Oscar said they remind him of his abuela's baking. Juno saved one in her bag for later.

Frankie's been weird again lately, not bad weird just... off. He mentioned his ex keeps texting him and he doesn't know how to handle it. Said it ended badly a few months ago. I just listened, didn't have advice really.

Orgo midterm tomorrow. I've studied more for this than anything in my life. Beatrice organized a last-minute cram session that was actually helpful. Even Nico showed up on time for once, proving he does understand the gravity of the situation.
Can't sleep. Molecules dancing in my head. If I dream about chair conformations I'm going to scream.

MIDTERM DONE. No idea how it went. Either I passed with flying colors or failed spectacularly, no in between. Walked out feeling empty. Beatrice immediately started analyzing which questions were hardest. I cannot engage with that energy right now.

Celebratory post-midterm lunch at Sunrise Diner. The whole study group went. Even Dmitri seemed relaxed for once. Nico ordered an absurd amount of pancakes and actually finished them. Where does it go.
The bus stop speech guy was practicing something about renewable energy today. His audience (me and one other person) did not seem impressed but he was undeterred. Passion is important I guess.
Zara invited me to some sociology department event about urban inequality. It was actually interesting? Free food and a speaker who knew what they were talking about. Academics can be cool sometimes.

Met Zara's friend Ivy again there. She's definitely intense but in a way I'm starting to appreciate. She challenged the speaker's methodology during Q&A which was bold. Speaker handled it gracefully but you could tell he was sweating.

Halloween is this week and people are already decorating. The dorm hall looks like a Spirit store exploded. Tobias went all out with fake cobwebs everywhere. Mira made him take down the fake blood though, said it was 'excessive.'
What am I going to be for Halloween. I have zero ideas and zero budget. Maybe I'll just wear all black and say I'm 'the concept of existential dread.' Very college.

Volleyball cancelled this week because the gym is being used for some alumni event. Hana sent disappointed emojis to the group chat but encouraged us to 'maintain our physical conditioning independently.' Sure Hana.

Work was chaos because everyone's ordering pumpkin spice everything. Oscar decided to lean into it and created a whole fall menu. The 'Autumn Harvest Latte' is just a regular latte with cinnamon and marketing.
Kwame's going as a camera for Halloween. Like literally, a cardboard box painted to look like a camera. He's been working on it all week. It's either genius or insane. Possibly both.

Beatrice's sister is visiting for Halloween apparently. She mentioned it during study group, seemed excited but also stressed about hosting. I forget what her sister's name is. Something with an A maybe?
Decided to go as a stressed college student for Halloween. Which requires no costume because I already am one. Kwame said that's 'depressingly relatable.' Fair.

HALLOWEEN. Theo's throwing a party obviously. Went with Zara and Ivy. Ivy was dressed as Ruth Bader Ginsburg which is exactly on-brand. Zara went as 'the concept of heartbreak' which was apparently just a lot of black and smudged makeup.

The party was packed. Saw volleyball people there, saw work people, saw random people I vaguely recognize from classes. Everyone blends together at parties. Kwame's camera costume was a hit, people kept posing with him.
Tobias showed up with Celeste and they seemed fine? Like whatever they were fighting about seems resolved. They were doing a couples costume, Bonnie and Clyde. Celeste made the outfits herself.
November already. Two months until winter break. That feels both very far away and very close. Time is doing something weird.

Orgo midterm results: B+!! I cannot believe it. Huang wrote 'consistent improvement' which is the kindest thing she's ever put on my work. Beatrice got an A+ because of course she did. Nico somehow got a B- despite attending maybe half the classes.

Dmitri got the highest grade again. Professor Huang asked if he'd considered doing undergraduate research with her lab. He seemed genuinely shocked that she knew his name. She knows everyone's name, she just chooses when to reveal it.
The pigeon council has dispersed. Haven't seen them in days. Either they migrated or they're planning something. Staying vigilant.

Spent the whole day in the library catching up on readings I'd been ignoring. Very productive. Slightly boring. The guy who lives there with his blanket nodded at me like a fellow resident. We're library neighbors now.
Juno didn't show up to her shift and Oscar couldn't reach her. Frankie and I handled it but it was rough. She texted later saying she was sick but something felt off. Hope she's actually okay.
Volleyball's back. First practice in a week and everyone was rusty. Hana made us do extra conditioning as 'punishment' for letting our skills lapse. My legs are going to hate me tomorrow.

Sienna was frustrated with herself during drills, kept messing up sets she normally nails. After practice I asked if she was good and she said 'just stuff going on, you know?' I nodded like I knew but I did not know.
What's the evolutionary advantage of having ticklish feet. It serves no purpose. Makes getting pedicures stressful. Biology is flawed.
Ran into Yuki at the vending machine at 3am. Neither of us acknowledged how weird it was to be getting Doritos at that hour. Silent solidarity between night owls.

Dr. Adebayo emailed about registration for next semester. Already??? I haven't processed this semester yet. She suggested 'planning ahead' which is advice I will definitely ignore until the last minute.

Professor Klein's class has a waitlist apparently because someone dropped. My friend back home who's suffering in it sent me her notes asking for help and I genuinely couldn't follow them. That class is on another level.
Worked with Frankie today and he was back to normal, joking around with customers. Asked him how things were going with the ex situation and he said he blocked her number. Clean break. Good for him.
Oscar noticed the shift too and seemed pleased. He's not very expressive but you can tell when he's in a good mood because he hums while doing inventory. Today there was humming.

Zara texted that she saw Malik with someone else at a coffee shop and she's 'totally fine with it' which probably means she's not fine with it. Offered ice cream again. She declined, said she's 'processing maturely.' Sure.
The campus squirrels have gotten bolder. One literally climbed onto my backpack while I was sitting on a bench. I made eye contact with it. It did not look away. Asserting dominance.
Study group at the library today. We've developed a system where Beatrice explains things, Dmitri confirms if she's right, Nico asks questions, and I take notes. Division of labor.

Beatrice mentioned Mr. Odom, my old high school teacher, would be proud of how I'm doing in orgo. I forgot I mentioned him once when talking about why I got interested in science. She remembers everything.
Why do they call it a building if it's already built. Should be called a built. English is a scam language.
Game day against Whitmore Hall. They're supposedly undefeated which is intimidating but Hana says that just means they have further to fall. Her motivational tactics are aggressive but effective.
WE BEAT THEM. Not even close actually, we played out of our minds. Reggie had like ten blocks and Sienna's sets were untouchable. The look on the Whitmore captain's face was priceless.
Hana cried a little after the game which made everyone else emotional. It's just intramural volleyball but it feels like more when you're in it. Team sports hit different.
Celebration at Theo's because where else. His housemates made a banner that said 'VOLLEYBALL CHAMPIONS' even though we're not champions yet, just undefeated for now. The enthusiasm is appreciated.
Juno texted asking if I could cover her shift tomorrow. She's been absent a lot lately. I said yes because I need the money anyway but I'm a little worried about her.
Long shift at The Grind. The morning rush was brutal but tips were good. Oscar bought everyone lunch as a thank you for handling the chaos. He's a good boss honestly.

One of the regulars, a grad student named Victor, always orders a chai latte with extra foam. He's working on his dissertation about medieval trade routes and sometimes tells me random facts while I make his drink. Today I learned about the spice trade.

Kwame's photography project got selected for some student showcase. He's been insufferably proud about it all day and honestly he deserves to be. The photos of me at The Grind actually look really good.
The heating in our room is either off or volcanic. No middle setting. Right now it's volcanic and Kwame and I are both in t-shirts with the window open. In November. Dorm life.
Professor Huang announced a surprise quiz for next class. The audacity. The betrayal. Beatrice immediately started organizing an emergency study session. I admire her crisis response.

Tobias threw a random mid-week hangout. Nothing big, just a few people watching bad movies. His commentary made the terrible acting actually funny. Celeste made popcorn with weird toppings - parmesan and nutritional yeast. Surprisingly good?

Quiz wasn't as bad as expected. Huang looked almost disappointed that people were prepared. Beatrice's emergency session saved lives. Nico even managed a passing score which he celebrated like he won the lottery.
There's a food truck festival on campus this weekend and my wallet is going to suffer. Saw a truck that only serves grilled cheese in like thirty different variations. This is my calling.

Went to the food truck festival with Zara and ate way too much. The grilled cheese truck lived up to the hype. Also tried Korean BBQ tacos which changed my life. We sat on the quad for hours just people-watching.
Zara mentioned she's been talking to someone new. Didn't give details but she had that smile people get when they're interested in someone. Good for her honestly, moving on is healthy.
The chess guy was at the festival playing speed chess against all challengers. He was undefeated when I left. Someone should tell Dmitri about this, would love to see that matchup.
Layla and I met to finalize our psych paper. It's actually done and I'm proud of it. Good data, clear conclusions, properly cited. This is what competence feels like.
She's applying for some summer research program and asked if I'd look over her application essay. It was already really good but she wanted fresh eyes. Helped her tighten a few paragraphs.
The pigeon is back. Singular again. Where did its friend go. Was there a pigeon breakup. I'm projecting human drama onto birds.
Kwame had his friend from Connecticut visit this weekend. Nice guy, they're childhood friends apparently. They spoke in this shorthand that I couldn't follow but their dynamic was fun to observe.
Couldn't sleep so I walked to the 24-hour convenience store at 2am. The person working there didn't even blink. They've seen things. I bought chips and regret.

Volleyball practice was focused on defense today. My diving has improved, fewer face-plants. Hana made us watch film of our own mistakes which was humbling. Seeing yourself mess up in slow motion is a special kind of pain.
Reggie suggested we do a team bonding thing outside of practice. Bowling was proposed. Sienna seemed skeptical but agreed. We're going this weekend apparently.
Oscar asked if I wanted more shifts during the holiday season since we'll be busy. More money but also more work. Said yes because textbooks aren't going to pay for themselves.
Frankie taught me his technique for handling impatient customers. It's mostly just smiling aggressively while moving faster. Works surprisingly well. Oscar calls it 'weaponized pleasantness.'

Juno finally came in and she seemed off. Talked to her in the back and she admitted she's been struggling with anxiety, hard to leave her room some days. She's seeing a counselor now though. Glad she told me.
There's a campus therapy dog event next week and I'm going to attend specifically to pet dogs. Self-care. Medical reasons. Definitely going.
Why do we park in driveways but drive on parkways. Who decided this. Was it intentional chaos or just negligence.

Mira called a floor meeting about 'community standards' which is code for 'someone keeps leaving dirty dishes in the common room.' She was trying to be diplomatic but you could feel the passive aggression. Kwame and I are innocent this time.
The meeting went long because Tobias turned it into a debate about personal freedom versus collective responsibility. Mira looked exhausted. Being an RA seems like a thankless job honestly.

Studied at the library with Dmitri. He helped me with a concept I couldn't grasp no matter how many times I read the textbook. Took him five minutes to explain what I'd been struggling with for days. Some people just have that gift.

He mentioned he's thinking about changing his major from pre-med to chemistry research. His parents want him to be a doctor but he's more interested in the theory side. Sounded like he hadn't told many people.

The therapy dog event was AMAZING. There were like fifteen dogs of all sizes. I spent thirty minutes with a golden retriever named Biscuit and all my stress melted away. Should be required for all students.
Zara was there too and we bonded over a corgi named Professor Waffles. She asked why dogs can't be in dorms and I don't have a good answer. The system is flawed.
Team bowling night was chaos in the best way. Turns out most of us are terrible at bowling. Hana, predictably, was good. Reggie somehow got three gutter balls in a row and blamed the lane.
Sienna won which surprised everyone including her. She was in a much better mood than she's been lately, actually laughing and joking around. Whatever was bothering her seems to have eased up.

After bowling we got pizza and talked about non-volleyball stuff for once. Learned that Reggie wants to be a physical therapist, Hana's majoring in accounting of all things, and Sienna's studying communications.

Coach Tran actually spoke more than two sentences today at practice. He gave this whole speech about finals coming up and maintaining work-life balance. Didn't know he had that in him. Felt almost parental.

Grandma Lucia's weekly call was extra long today. She's planning Thanksgiving dinner and needed to tell me every single dish she's making in elaborate detail. I'm not even going home for Thanksgiving but the vicarious food descriptions were nice.

Speaking of Thanksgiving, Kwame invited me to his family's thing since I'm not traveling. They're in Connecticut which is doable by train. Actually considering it. Might be weird but also might be nice.

Oscar's already decorating The Grind for the holidays. November isn't even over but there are twinkle lights everywhere. Frankie complained about corporate festivity creep but Oscar just handed him more lights to hang.

Juno seems to be doing better. She came to her shift on time and seemed more present. We didn't talk about it directly but I made sure she knew I'm around if she needs anything. Sometimes that's enough.

The complicated drink guy came in and his order has somehow gotten MORE complicated. Now there's a temperature specification and something about the type of milk foam. Frankie and I exchanged a look but we delivered.
What if socks had left and right versions. Would that be better or worse. My brain does this at 2am.

Zara officially introduced me to Andre at Theo's party. That's the guy she's been talking to. He's a junior, philosophy major, very intense eye contact. They seem good together, different energy than her and Malik.
Theo's parties are becoming a weekly fixture in my life. I'm not sure when that happened but here we are. At least it's consistent social interaction without having to plan anything.
Saw Ivy there being intense at someone about economic policy. The guy seemed overwhelmed. Zara just watched fondly like 'yes, this is my friend, this is what she does.' Good dynamics.

Sunday recovery day. Did absolutely nothing productive and I refuse to feel bad about it. Kwame and I watched five episodes of the pastry detective show. The season finale is next week and I'm emotionally unprepared.
The library was empty because everyone's recovering from the weekend. Claimed my third floor spot back from blanket guy who was mysteriously absent. Victory.
Professor Huang announced that the final is cumulative. The whole class groaned in unison. She seemed to enjoy our suffering. 'This is organic chemistry,' she said, like that explains the cruelty.
Ronan held a Q&A session about the final and it was packed. Standing room only. He handled it like a pro, answering questions for two hours straight. That man deserves a raise or at least a nap.
Beatrice is already in full finals mode. She's created a color-coded study schedule that spans the next three weeks. I asked her to share it and it's intimidating but useful. Organization by osmosis.
The squirrel army has returned in force. I counted seven of them on the quad today coordinating their approach to anyone with food. They've evolved. We're not safe.
Volleyball's semi-finals next week and Hana's intensity has reached new levels. Practice ran an extra hour. My entire body is sore but the drills were effective. We might actually win this thing.
Sienna stayed after again to work on plays with Hana. Their chemistry on the court is unreal, they can practically read each other's minds. Watching them strategize is impressive.
Accepted Kwame's Thanksgiving invite. His mom apparently insists on feeding any 'strays' which is a weird way to describe me but I'll take the home-cooked meal. Better than dining hall turkey.
Called mom to tell her my plans and she was relieved I won't be alone. She wants FaceTime during dinner which is going to be awkward at someone else's table but I said yes anyway.

Work was slow so Oscar let us practice latte art. Mine looked like a blob. Frankie's was actually a recognizable heart. Juno accidentally made something that looked like a ghost, which she's claiming was intentional.

Victor the chai latte grad student asked my opinion on his dissertation title options. I know nothing about medieval trade routes but I picked the one that sounded coolest. He seemed satisfied with my completely uninformed input.
The dining hall is going all out for a pre-Thanksgiving feast tomorrow. I'm skeptical but also hungry enough to be optimistic. At minimum there will be pie. Pie is hard to ruin.

Last study group before break. Beatrice brought a cake she made herself with a fondant molecule on top. Genuinely impressive baking skills on top of everything else she's good at. Some people really have it together.

Dmitri opened up more about his family situation. His dad's still in Belarus, complicated political reasons he didn't fully explain. It's just him and his mom here. That explains some of his independence.
Pre-Thanksgiving feast was... fine. Not bad. The pie was good as predicted. Sat with Tobias and Celeste since Kwame was packing. They're going to her family's place, both seemed nervous about it.
Campus is emptying out. Half the dorm is already gone. The quiet is nice but also eerie. Saw Yuki in the hall and she confirmed she's staying too. 'Too far to fly home,' she said. Fair.

Train to Connecticut with Kwame tomorrow morning. He's more excited to introduce me to his mom's cooking than to his actual family which is funny. 'You don't understand,' he keeps saying. 'Her mac and cheese.'
The train ride was nice actually. Three hours of watching suburbs turn into smaller towns. Kwame slept most of it but I just looked out the window thinking about stuff. Peaceful.

Kwame's family is overwhelming in the best way. His mom hugged me immediately, his dad shook my hand firmly, his little sister asked me approximately one million questions. Their house smells like cinnamon.
The mac and cheese lived up to the hype. Everything lived up to the hype. I ate until I couldn't move and Kwame's mom kept trying to feed me more. This is what hospitality looks like.

FaceTimed mom during dinner and she cried seeing me at someone else's table but in a happy way? Parents are complicated. Grandma Lucia wanted to know if the food was as good as hers. I lied and said no.
Kwame's sister Grace is sixteen and has strong opinions about everything. She interrogated me about college life for an hour. I tried to be honest but also not scare her. 'It's fine' covers a lot.
Slept in Kwame's childhood bedroom surrounded by his old posters. He was really into superheroes apparently. The room is a time capsule of 2018. He seemed embarrassed but I found it endearing.
Leftover breakfast was arguably better than the actual dinner. Cold mac and cheese hits different. Kwame's mom sent us back with containers of food that will definitely not fit in our mini fridge.
Train back was full of other college students returning from break. Everyone had that same well-fed, slightly tired look. National mood.
Dorm still mostly empty but people are trickling back. The heating decided to break while we were gone so it's freezing. Maintenance says they'll fix it tomorrow. Wearing three sweaters to bed.
Back to reality. Finals in two weeks. Semi-finals for volleyball this week. Work needs me for extra shifts. When did I sign up for all this.

The Grind was SLAMMED with people needing caffeine after break. Oscar looked like he was going to cry from stress. All hands on deck situation. I made more lattes in four hours than I usually make in a week.
Frankie was in crisis management mode, somehow calm despite the chaos. Oscar relies on him more than he probably should. They've developed this shorthand communication that's almost telepathic.

Juno handled the rush like a champ. Big improvement from when she started. She even made a joke about it after, said the anxiety of coffee rush is different from her actual anxiety. Humor as coping, respect.
Margaret the regular came in and immediately noticed I looked tired. Grandma energy from a stranger. She ordered her usual and told me to 'take care of myself.' I will try, Margaret.
Volleyball practice was light since semis are in two days. Hana didn't want us exhausted. Just ran through plays and did some light conditioning. The calm before the storm.

Tobias threw a small gathering to celebrate surviving Thanksgiving with Celeste's family. Apparently it was 'a lot' but they're still together so that's good. He reenacted some conversations and I was crying laughing.
Why do we say 'heads up' when we mean 'duck.' Shouldn't it be 'heads down.' The warning is counterproductive. Language is chaos.

Mira is stressed about finals while also doing RA duties. Saw her in the hall looking exhausted and offered her coffee. She accepted which felt like a breakthrough in our formal nodding-only relationship.

Kwame got a noise complaint from Mira because his video call with Grace was too loud at 11pm. She had to write him up officially this time. He's upset but trying to play it cool. His scholarship paranoia is through the roof now.
Semi-finals tomorrow. Can't sleep. Keep running through plays in my head. Hana sent a 'get rest, we've got this' message to the group chat which was nice but did not help me rest.

SEMI-FINALS WON. It was close, went to three sets, but we pulled through. Sienna was MVP honestly, her sets in the third set were clutch. Finals next week against Crawford, the only team that's beaten us.
The team was buzzing after the win. Hana was already analyzing film of Crawford while everyone else celebrated. She's locked in. Her dedication is honestly inspiring even if it's a lot sometimes.
Theo's party was more subdued tonight, finals stress dampening the usual energy. Still went but left early. Need to actually study. Being responsible feels wrong but necessary.
Study group emergency session. Beatrice has somehow become more intense. She's running practice problems like a drill sergeant. Nico looks scared. Dmitri is thriving. I'm somewhere in between.
Professor Huang held review sessions that were actually helpful. She almost smiled once. Almost. The class is too scared to point it out but we all saw it.
The library is now 24/7 for finals period and it shows. People camping out with sleeping bags. The vending machines are constantly empty. This is academia.
Ran into the guy who lives in the library and he looked rough. We nodded at each other like soldiers passing in a trench. Finals solidarity.
December. The final month. The end times. Dramatic? Yes. Accurate? Also yes.

Work is decorated to the extreme now. Oscar added a whole hot cocoa menu. The festive drinks are complicated but tips are better so I'm not complaining. Made approximately fifty peppermint mochas today.
Frankie wore a Santa hat ironically and then kept it on all shift unironically. Oscar took a photo for the shop's Instagram. Juno added reindeer antlers. I remained hatless and was judged for it.
Zara and Andre are official now apparently. She posted a photo and everything. Ivy commented 'finally' which suggests everyone knew before I did. Happy for her though, she seems genuinely happy.
Can't feel my brain. Studied for six hours straight. Beatrice's flashcards are burned into my retinas. If I see another reaction mechanism I might scream.
Kwame brought me food because I forgot to eat. Good roommate. He's stressed too but handling it better than me. His finals are papers not exams which seems both easier and harder.
Volleyball finals tomorrow. The timing is terrible with exams but we've come this far. Hana's final pep talk was emotional. 'Whatever happens, we did something special this season.' I believe her.
CHAMPIONSHIP GAME. We lost but only by two points. Crawford was just slightly better. Sienna cried. Hana cried. I didn't cry but I was close. Still proud of what we accomplished.
Post-game Sunrise Diner tradition, even in defeat. The pancakes tasted the same whether you win or lose. Reggie gave a surprisingly moving speech about teamwork. Who knew he had that depth.
Coach Tran spoke more than he has all season. Told us we exceeded expectations, that this team had something special. Coming from him that meant a lot. He's not one for empty praise.
Hana wants to do a team reunion next semester. Already planning. Even in the aftermath of loss she's organizing. Leadership is just part of who she is I guess.
Back to studying. The game was a good distraction but now reality hits. Orgo final in four days. Psych final in three. Everything is fine. Totally fine.
The library has achieved maximum capacity. Every seat taken. People sitting on floors. I found a spot in a stairwell and I'm claiming it. This is my stairwell now.
Beatrice organized one final study session before the orgo exam. We've been through war together this semester. Whatever happens, these people helped me survive.
Nico brought his cookies again. He finally admitted the secret ingredient is brown butter. Revolutionary. The cookies were stress-eaten within minutes.
Dmitri walked me through the hardest concepts one more time. His patience is endless. If he doesn't become a research scientist the world is wasting talent.
Can't sleep again. Molecules again. This is my life now. I am one with the carbon chains. The functional groups speak to me.
PSYCH FINAL DONE. Went well I think. All that work with Layla paid off. The essay questions were fair. Now just orgo standing between me and freedom.
Layla and I celebrated with mediocre dining hall food. We survived the semester as partners. She's applying to be a TA next year which she'd be great at.
Why is it called a 'pair of pants' when it's one item. We don't say 'a pair of shirts.' The plural is built in. Make it make sense.
Last shift at The Grind before I leave for break. Oscar gave everyone small bonuses as holiday gifts. Not much but the gesture was nice. He's a good boss.
Said goodbye to the regulars for the semester. Margaret hugged me. Victor wished me luck on finals. Even complicated drink guy seemed almost warm. The Grind is a weird little community.
Frankie's staying in town for break, working extra shifts. He's saving for a trip somewhere next summer. Juno's flying home to California, seemed genuinely excited to see her family.
Tomorrow is orgo. THE final. The big one. Beatrice sent a 'we've got this' message to the group chat. Dmitri replied with a chemistry pun that nobody understood. Classic Dmitri.
Kwame wished me luck and promised to be quiet while I sleep. He's already done with his finals, just has to turn in a paper. Must be nice. Different academic universes.
The final. Three hours. Felt like thirty minutes and ten years simultaneously. Huang watched us the whole time which was unnerving. But I knew stuff. I actually knew stuff.
Walked out feeling cautiously optimistic. Beatrice was already analyzing which questions were tricky. Nico looked shell-shocked but thought he passed. Dmitri was typically calm.
FREEDOM. It's done. Whatever grade I get, it's done. This semester is DONE. The relief is physical. I could sleep for a week.

Celebration dinner with the study group at a nice restaurant Beatrice picked. We all cleaned up, looked like actual adults. Toasted to surviving. Nico paid for dessert which was surprisingly generous.
Beatrice is going to shadow at a hospital over break. Dmitri's staying to do research with Professor Huang. Nico's 'vibing at home' which tracks. We'll reconvene for orgo 2 apparently.
One more Theo party before everyone scatters. The energy was different, lighter. People actually relaxed instead of stressed. End of semester magic.
Zara and Andre were being cute in a corner. Ivy was debating someone about something intense as always. Tobias did karaoke badly. Normal chaos.
Said proper goodbyes to the volleyball crew. We have a group chat that will definitely stay active. Already planning pickup games for next semester. This team became real friends.
Sienna seemed happier than she's been in weeks. I finally asked if she was okay and she said 'yeah, finally sorted some stuff out.' Didn't elaborate but seemed genuine.
Packing to go home is harder than packing to come here. Too much stuff accumulated. Where did all these things come from. I don't remember acquiring half of this.
Kwame and I did our end of semester roommate check-in. We work well together. Already confirmed we're rooming again next semester. Some things just click.
The pigeon was at the window when I was packing. Final goodbye I guess. We've shared a semester together. It watched me struggle to close my suitcase. Judgment in its eyes.
Mira knocked to do final room inspections. She seemed less RA-robot-mode, actually asked how finals went. Progress. Kwame still tensed up when she appeared though. The write-up trauma.
Why is it called rush hour when nothing moves. Cities are built on lies.
Last night in the dorm for the semester. Quiet. Most people already gone. Yuki waved goodbye in the hall, said 'see you next year' which is technically tomorrow but I knew what she meant.
Dad picked me up which was a surprise. Usually it's mom. Long drive home but we talked more than usual. He asked real questions about school. Growth.
Home. My childhood room feels smaller. Or I feel bigger. Mom made my favorite dinner. Grandma Lucia is coming over tomorrow. The holidays officially begin.
Slept for twelve hours straight. My body just shut down. College exhaustion is a specific kind of tired that only home can cure.
Grandma arrived with enough food to feed an army. She pinched my cheeks and said I looked 'too thin' as predicted. The cookies she sent were apparently just a preview. More incoming.
Cameron came over and we caught up for hours. His semester was rough too. We complained together like old times. High school friendship is different, more comfortable somehow.
He asked about my social life at school and I realized how many people I've met. Rattled off names and he was impressed. 'You actually made friends,' he said. 'Proud of you.'

Texted Sienna to check in and we ended up talking for a while. She mentioned she's been dealing with ex drama - apparently he works at some campus coffee place and she keeps running into him. Awkward situation.
Mom is in full holiday decorating mode. The house looks like Christmas exploded. I was recruited to help hang lights and only fell off the ladder once. Minor injuries.
Dad and I watched football together which is our version of bonding. We don't talk much but we share a couch. That's something.
Grades started posting and I'm anxiously refreshing my student portal. Psych came in: A-. Layla texted celebrating, she got an A. Our project was solid.
ORGO GRADE: B+!! I cannot believe it. Huang gave me a B+. The same grade as the midterm. Consistent improvement? Consistent survival. I'll take it.
Group chat exploded. Beatrice: A+ (obvious). Dmitri: A+ (also obvious). Nico: B- (miracle). We all made it through. Celebration emojis everywhere.
Called Kwame to share the news. He did well too. We're both on track. The relief of not failing anything is genuinely overwhelming.
Why do they call it a TV 'set' when there's only one. Who has a set of TVs. Old terminology haunting us.
Holiday shopping with mom. The mall is chaos but she wanted company. Helped pick out gifts for relatives I barely remember. 'They'll love it,' she assured me about everything.
Got Kwame a photography book as a thank you for being a good roommate. Found a small Westbrook hoodie for Grace because she asked me a million questions about college. Gifts are hard.
Mr. Odom, my old high school chemistry teacher, reached out to say he heard I was doing well in orgo. Word travels in small towns. He seemed genuinely proud which hit me in the feelings.

Zara sent a photo of her family's holiday setup. Andre's meeting them next week. Big step. Ivy commented that she'll 'interrogate him on Zara's behalf' which sounds threatening but is probably a joke.
The volleyball group chat is planning a New Year's video call. Hana's already made a Google Calendar invite. She cannot turn off the organizing instinct. I respect it.
Sienna confirmed she's coming to the call, said she'll be at her aunt's house but will make it work. She seems lighter lately, the ex drama must be resolving.
Home is comfortable but I'm already kind of missing school? Is that weird? Missing the dorm, the routine, the people. Four weeks feels long.
Grandma Lucia is teaching me to make her famous sauce recipe. It's a multi-day process apparently. Day one: acquiring ingredients and listening to stories about 'the old country.'
She's 84 and still sharper than me. Quizzed me on school stuff and actually understood most of it. 'In my day, women couldn't even go to university,' she said. 'You make the most of it.'
Why is 'abbreviation' such a long word. Seems counterproductive. English continues to make zero sense.
Snow!! First real snow of the season. Everything is white and quiet. Built a snowman with the neighbor kids because apparently I'm twelve again. No regrets.
Mom made hot chocolate after and we watched old holiday movies. Simple pleasures. This is what home is supposed to feel like.
Oscar texted checking in on everyone. He sent a photo of the holiday decorations at The Grind. Frankie's wearing elf ears in the background. Some things don't change.
Cameron's having a small New Year's party and invited me. Just high school people, low-key. Said yes but also wondering how different it'll feel now that I've changed. If I've changed.
Sauce day two: the simmering. Grandma supervised while I stirred. She corrected my technique approximately fifty times. 'More gentle,' she kept saying. 'You're not fighting it.'
Dad asked about my plans for next semester. We actually had a conversation about the future. He suggested considering internships. 'Think ahead,' he said. Very dad advice.
The chess guy from the quad has a website apparently. Dmitri found it and sent the link. He's a retired physics professor who plays to stay sharp. Mystery solved.
Beatrice sent her hospital shadowing photos. She looks genuinely happy in scrubs. Some people just know what they want. I admire that clarity.
Why is there a 'D' in fridge but not in refrigerator. What happened to consistency.
Holiday shopping complete. Wrapped everything badly. My wrapping looks like a toddler did it. Mom offered to help and then just redid everything while I 'supervised.'
Juno texted from California saying she's decided not to transfer. Going to give it another semester. The Grind crew will stay intact. Good news.
Kwame's family photos from Connecticut are chaotic in the best way. Grace is doing bunny ears behind everyone. His mom looks exasperated but happy. Good people.
Winter solstice. Shortest day of the year. From here on out more light. That feels metaphorical but I'm not going to force it.
Layla emailed that our psych paper got selected for some undergraduate research showcase. We have to present in February. Unexpected but cool.
Sauce day three: the final product. Grandma approved. She actually said 'not bad' which is high praise from her. We made enough to feed the family for months.
Relatives arriving for Christmas. Cousins I barely remember, aunts who pinch cheeks, uncles who ask about 'the girlfriend' which, no. Family chaos commencing.
One of my cousins is also in college and we bonded in the corner while adults talked about boring adult stuff. She's at a school in Oregon, studying environmental science.
Christmas Eve dinner was overwhelming in the best way. Too much food, too much noise, too much family. But also warm. This is what the holidays are about I guess.
Grandma Lucia gave me $100 'for books' with a wink that clearly meant 'for whatever you want.' Grandparents are the best loophole in the system.
Christmas morning. Presents. Coffee. Mom crying at everything. Dad pretending not to be emotional. The usual.
Got some practical stuff - clothes, gift cards - and one surprise: a nice watch from my parents. 'For adulthood,' they said. Pressure but thoughtful.
Video called Kwame to compare Christmas hauls. Grace interrupted to show me her new camera - apparently photography runs in the family now. She was very enthusiastic.
Post-Christmas food coma. Ate too much. No regrets. This is the way.
Hana sent a 'Merry Christmas from your captain' message to the volleyball chat. Reggie responded at midnight which means he was also awake thinking about volleyball. These are my people.
Sienna posted a photo of her family's tree. Simple caption about being grateful. She seems to be in a really good place. The ex situation must finally be behind her.
Day after Christmas is weirdly empty. The buildup is over. Now just waiting for New Year's. Liminal time.
Why is New Year's Eve such a big deal. It's just another night. But also I'm going to Cameron's party so clearly I'm a hypocrite.

Volleyball video call! Everyone looked happy and rested. Hana already talked about next semester's training schedule. Sienna was at her aunt's house like she said, looked relaxed. We set the call for match point and she spiked it, winning energy.
Reggie shared his physical therapist school plans in detail. He's actually researched programs. Impressed by his direction. I still don't know what I want to do with my life.
Cameron's party prep: he's stressed about hosting for some reason. It's just like twelve people. I offered to come early and help set up. What are friends for.
Helped Cameron arrange snacks in 'aesthetically pleasing' ways which apparently matters now. When did we grow up. Why.
New Year's Eve. Cameron's party was nice. Mostly people I remember from high school, some I didn't recognize anymore. Everyone's changed but also the same. Weird.
Midnight came and went. Cheered with old friends. Texted college friends. Straddling two worlds. Both feel real.
First day of the new year and I slept until 2pm. Strong start. Mom made her 'good luck' meal - black-eyed peas and cornbread. Family tradition.
Thinking about next semester. Orgo 2, more work at The Grind, volleyball if they'll have me back. Structure is forming. It's almost exciting.
Kwame sent his New Year's resolutions which included 'win photography award' and 'teach roommate to appreciate good music.' I'm presumably the roommate. Fair.
Why do we call it 'catching' a cold when we clearly don't want it. Should be 'getting caught by a cold.' The cold is the aggressor here.
Last few days at home feeling bittersweet. Ready to go back but also want to freeze time here. Can't have both. That's life I guess.
Grandma Lucia left after one final cheek pinch and reminder to eat more. Promised to call every Sunday like always. She's the constant.
Packing for the return trip. Somehow I have MORE stuff than I came with. Where does it come from. Physics is a lie.
Mom packed 'snacks for the journey' which is enough food to survive an apocalypse. I'm not complaining but the suitcase is struggling.
Last dinner at home. Dad got quiet which means he's feeling things. Mom was openly emotional. Even the dog seemed to know I was leaving.
Tomorrow I go back. Next semester begins. Whatever happens, I survived the first one. That's something. That's actually a lot.

Back on campus. The dorm smells exactly the same - that specific combination of industrial cleaner and desperation. Kwame beat me here somehow, already has his photography stuff spread across his desk.
what classes am I taking this semester again
Right, orgo 2. The sequel nobody asked for. Also signed up for a stats class which feels like a mistake already. And some gen ed about film that should be easy.
Kwame's already planning his next photography project. Something about 'capturing liminal spaces on campus.' I nodded like I understood what that meant.
The dining hall hasn't changed. Same mystery meat, same questionable vegetables. At least the consistency is comforting in a way.

Saw Tobias in the hall and he immediately launched into a story about his break. Something about his cousin's wedding and a karaoke incident. I got the highlights version which was still twenty minutes.
Celeste wasn't with him which is unusual. He said she's not back yet, coming tomorrow. They seemed solid last semester so probably nothing.
The pigeon is BACK. Same window. Same stare. We've been apart for weeks and it found me immediately. This is either loyalty or a threat.
First day back at The Grind tomorrow. Oscar texted the schedule and I'm opening which means 6am which means pain.
when did I start working at The Grind
September feels like forever ago. Wild that I've been doing this for almost five months now. Time is fake.
Walked around campus to remember where everything is. The science building is still unreasonably far from everything. Some things never change.
Professor Whiskers the campus cat is alive and well. Saw him lounging near the library entrance like he owns the place. He probably does.
Orgo 2 starts Wednesday. Huang is teaching it again because of course she is. At least I know what to expect now. Beatrice already sent study group scheduling options.
Opening shift was brutal but good to be back. Oscar was there setting up and we caught up a bit. He spent break with family in Arizona, showed me pictures of his niece.
Frankie came in for his shift looking rough. Said break was 'complicated' but didn't elaborate. He's back to his normal self by the end though, joking with regulars.
Juno's not back until next week apparently. Oscar said she's taking a few extra days with family. Hope she's doing okay mentally, she was struggling last semester.
what's the name of that regular who's always talking about medieval stuff
Victor! Right. He came in today, same chai latte order. Said his dissertation defense is in March. The man is living on caffeine and primary sources.
Why is January so long. It's been January for approximately three years. February needs to hurry up.
First orgo 2 class. Huang walked in, wrote 'CARBONYL CHEMISTRY' on the board, and said 'this will be your life now.' Reassuring.
Study group reunited. Beatrice has new highlighters - apparently she got a 48-pack for Christmas. Nico was late as always. Dmitri looked tan which is confusing for someone from Belarus.
Dmitri went to Florida with his mom over break. He seemed slightly sunburned and confused about why Americans love theme parks so much. Fair question.
what's Beatrice's cat's name
Molecule! She showed new pictures. The cat has gotten fatter. Beatrice blames her sister who visited and apparently fed it constantly.
Volleyball group chat is active again. Hana's already talking about spring intramural signups. The woman does not rest.
Sienna confirmed she's playing again which is good, we need her sets. She seemed more relaxed in the chat than she was at the end of last semester.
Reggie sent a video of himself doing blocking drills alone in some gym. Captioned 'never stop grinding.' This man is intense.
Stats class might actually kill me. The professor talks like we already know everything and moves at lightspeed. I'm lost and it's day one.
The film class is the opposite - super chill professor who spent the first day talking about his favorite movies. We're starting with Hitchcock which seems reasonable.
Kwame convinced me to watch some art house film with him. Two hours of my life I'll never get back. He loved it. I fell asleep twice.
If a tree falls in the forest and no one's around, does it make a sound? More importantly, if I fail stats and no one's around, did it really happen?
Mira did start-of-semester rounds and was... almost friendly? Asked how my break was. Kwame froze up when she knocked, still traumatized from the write-up.
She lingered at the door longer than usual talking to Kwame specifically. Asked about his photography project. Interesting.
Zara's back and we got lunch. She and Andre are still going strong, talked about him for like thirty minutes. The philosophy major is growing on her apparently.
Ivy joined us halfway through, immediately started arguing with Andre about Kant. Zara just watched fondly. These are the people I know now.
remind me who Zara was dating before Andre
Right, Malik. Haven't seen him around this semester. Maybe he's avoiding places Zara goes. Smart.
Work was slow so Oscar let us do inventory together. Exciting stuff. He's very particular about how things are organized but in a way that makes sense once you learn the system.
Margaret came in and immediately asked if I had a 'nice young person' in my life. Grandma energy from all angles. I said I was focusing on school which is both true and an excuse.
Frankie was talking to Oscar in the back for a while. Couldn't hear what about but Frankie looked serious. When he came out he just said 'life stuff' and changed the subject.
