There's a new coffee shop that opened across from campus and Oscar's stressed about competition. I told him The Grind has loyal regulars. He nodded but still seemed worried.
Checked out the competition with Kwame. It's very aesthetic, lots of plants and exposed brick. Coffee was fine but not better than ours. Overpriced too.
The squirrels have formed a winter alliance. Saw six of them huddled together on the quad. They looked at me with collective intent. I walked faster.
Volleyball practice officially starts next week. Hana sent the schedule and it's intense. Two practices a week plus optional weekend sessions. 'Optional.'
Orgo 2 is already harder than orgo 1. Carbonyl chemistry is not my friend. Beatrice is making new flashcards at an alarming rate.
Ronan's holding office hours again. The man is a saint. His explanations are the only reason I survived last semester.
Tobias and Celeste had another fight. Could hear it through the walls. Something about her spending too much time in the art studio. Dorm drama continues.
what did I get on my orgo 1 final
Right, B+. Huang's handwriting is seared into my memory. Need to at least maintain that or she'll know I got lazy over break.
Saw Kwame and Mira talking in the common room. Like actually talking, not RA-to-resident talking. She was laughing at something he said. Kwame. Making Mira laugh. What.
Asked him about it and he got weirdly defensive. 'We were just talking about photography.' Sure, Kwame. Sure.
Why do we say 'after dark' when it's actually during dark. It's not after, it's during. Language is a collective hallucination.
First volleyball practice of the semester. Everyone's rusty but the energy was good. Sienna's sets are still elite. Hana yelled encouragingly.
After practice Sienna mentioned she's glad to have volleyball as a distraction. 'From what?' I asked. 'Just life stuff, you know.' I'm starting to think 'life stuff' is code for something specific.
Juno's back! She came into work looking better than last semester. Said the break helped, she's been working with her counselor on coping strategies.
The three of us - me, Juno, Frankie - working together feels like the proper Grind crew now. Oscar hovered less than usual which means he trusts us. Progress.
Grandma Lucia called. Sunday tradition. She's already planning what to send me for Valentine's Day. I told her she doesn't need to send anything. She ignored me.
The library is somehow already crowded and it's only week two. People are stressed. I'm stressed. We're all stressed together.
Dmitri helped me with stats concepts after study group. His math brain is unfair. He explained standard deviation like it was obvious. It was not obvious.
Cameron texted asking when he can visit. Spring break maybe? Would be cool to show him campus, introduce him to people.
The pastry detective show got renewed for another season. Kwame and I celebrated with snacks. Chef Inspector Moreau lives to solve another day.
what's the name of the diner we go to after volleyball
Sunrise Diner. The pancakes. I'm craving them now just thinking about it.
Game against Thornton Hall this weekend. They're new to the league so no idea what to expect. Hana's been watching their practice footage somehow. Dedication or stalking, unclear.
Tobias apologized to Celeste with a very public flower delivery to the dorm. She seemed embarrassed but also pleased. Young love is exhausting to witness.
Won against Thornton easily. They were nice but outmatched. Sienna had the game of her life, Reggie blocked everything. Team chemistry is clicking.
Sunrise Diner tradition continues. The waitress recognizes us now and brings extra syrup without asking. We've achieved regular status.
Hana made a speech about unity and momentum. She's really leaning into the captain role this semester. Nobody complained.
Saw Kwame leaving Mira's room at like 11pm. He claimed he was 'helping her with something.' The lies are getting weaker.
Confronted him about it. He admitted they've been 'hanging out.' I asked if that meant dating. He said 'it's complicated.' It's always complicated.
The irony of Kwame dating the RA who wrote him up is not lost on me. Life is a sitcom.
If you're waiting for a waiter, does that make you the waiter? These are the thoughts that keep me up at night.
Oscar was in crisis mode because one of the espresso machines broke again. The repair guy can't come until Thursday. We're down to one machine during morning rush. Chaos.
Frankie somehow kept everything running smoothly despite the equipment issues. Oscar called him 'indispensable' which made Frankie visibly uncomfortable with the praise.
Beatrice's study guides have evolved. She's now including mnemonic devices and memory palace techniques. I'm learning about learning while trying to learn. Meta.
Nico brought a new type of cookie - chocolate chip with sea salt. He's experimenting. We're his test subjects. No complaints.
The psych research showcase is in two weeks. Layla's been prepping our presentation obsessively. I should probably help more.
Practiced the presentation with Layla. It's actually good? Like we did real research and it means something. Weird feeling.
February tomorrow. January finally ended. It only took approximately seven years.
Zara invited me to her birthday thing next weekend. Small gathering at Andre's apartment. I've never been to his place, curious what a philosophy major's apartment looks like.
when is Zara's birthday
February 8th. Need to get a gift. What do you get someone who has strong opinions about capitalism? Probably not anything from Amazon.
Work was busy with the morning rush. Oscar's been promoting 'Valentine's drinks' and people are actually ordering them. The 'Cupid's Mocha' is just chocolate with sprinkles but marketing is powerful.
Frankie was in a weird mood, kept looking at his phone between orders. When I asked he said someone from his past keeps trying to reach out. 'I thought I was done with that drama.'
Mira joined us for dinner in the dining hall. Like sat with me and Kwame. As a person. Not as an RA. The world is shifting.
She's actually pretty cool when she's not in official mode. Funny even. Kwame was glowing. It was kind of sweet and also deeply weird.
Volleyball practice was rough. Coach Tran made us run drills until we couldn't feel our legs. He spoke in complete sentences which means he's invested now.
Sienna stayed after to work on timing with me. She mentioned she's been sleeping better lately. 'Finally dealing with stuff instead of avoiding it,' she said.
Orgo exam next week. First real test of the semester. Beatrice has scheduled four study sessions. FOUR.
The chess guy is back in the quad despite the cold. Saw him bundled up with hand warmers, still destroying challengers. Dedication.
Dmitri played him once. It was close but Dmitri lost. He seemed more intrigued than disappointed. 'I will return,' he said ominously.
Why is 'phonics' not spelled the way it sounds. The whole system is a lie.
Grandma's Valentine's package arrived early. Cookies shaped like hearts, a card with $50, and a note saying 'find someone nice.' Grandma please.
Shared the cookies at work. Oscar ate three before catching himself. 'Your grandmother is a treasure,' he said. Correct.
There's a girl in my stats class who always sits next to me. We've developed a silent rapport of shared confusion. Today she passed me her notes because I missed something. Progress.
Her name is Priya. Finally learned it after like four weeks of sitting together. She's a data science major which explains why she actually understands stats.
Kwame officially told me he and Mira are 'seeing each other.' I acted surprised even though I've known for weeks. Good friend behavior.
He's worried about what people will think - RA dating a resident on her floor. They're keeping it quiet for now. I promised not to say anything.
Game against Reynolds Hall. They were tough but we pulled through in the third set. Sienna was clutch under pressure. Hana cried happy tears again.
Someone from Reynolds mentioned they recognized Sienna. 'You dated that barista guy right?' Sienna's face went weird for a second before she changed the subject.
wait does Sienna know someone at The Grind
She mentioned her ex works at 'some campus coffee place' last semester. I work at a campus coffee place. The math is mathing but I don't want to assume.
Zara's birthday party was chill. Andre's apartment is exactly what you'd expect - lots of books, a poster of Nietzsche, mismatched furniture that somehow works.
Got her a nice journal and a gift card to that bookstore she likes. She seemed genuinely happy. Ivy got her a book about labor movements. Very on brand.
Andre made a speech about Zara that was either very romantic or very pretentious. Possibly both. She looked at him like he hung the moon.
Met some of Andre's philosophy friends. Very intense conversations about free will at a birthday party. Only at college.
Orgo exam tomorrow. Can't sleep. Carbonyl mechanisms dancing in my head. This is my life now.
Exam done. Felt okay? Huang's face revealed nothing as usual. Beatrice was confident, Nico looked shook, Dmitri was Dmitri.
Research showcase tomorrow. Layla and I did one final run-through. We're ready. Or as ready as we'll ever be.
SHOWCASE WENT GREAT. People asked good questions, seemed genuinely interested in our sleep deprivation research. One professor gave us her card.
Layla was in her element presenting. She's definitely going into academia or research. I'm still figuring things out but at least I can present now.
Valentine's Day approaching and the campus is covered in pink. The Grind is overwhelmed with couples ordering matching drinks. Oscar is stressed but profitable.
Working Valentine's Day which is fine, I don't have plans anyway. Frankie's working too. Juno has a date which surprised everyone including her.
Frankie made a comment about Valentine's being 'a trigger' for him but then laughed it off. Oscar just patted his shoulder without saying anything. Male communication.
Valentine's Day shift was chaos. So many lattes with heart foam. My arm hurts from steaming milk. But tips were excellent.
A couple broke up AT the counter while ordering. In front of us. Mid-transaction. Frankie handled it with grace I did not know he possessed.
Kwame and Mira did something together tonight. He was vague about details but came back looking happy. Good for them.
Why do they call it Valentine's Day when St. Valentine probably didn't even like chocolate. Historical marketing.
Orgo exam results: B. Not B+, just B. Huang wrote 'room for improvement.' Cold but fair.
Beatrice: A. Dmitri: A+. Nico: B-. The natural order is maintained.
Volleyball practice was intense because we have a big game coming up against Crawford. Rematch from the championship we lost. Hana is READY.
Sienna was distracted during practice, kept checking her phone. After I asked if she was okay and she said 'running into someone tomorrow I'd rather avoid.' Cryptic.
Work in the morning. Sienna said she usually avoids coming by The Grind. Why would she avoid... oh. OH.
who was Sienna dating before
She mentioned her ex worked at a campus coffee place and things ended badly. Frankie's ex also 'keeps reaching out' and 'ended badly.' The coincidences are piling up.
I'm probably overthinking this. There are multiple coffee places on campus. And lots of breakups happen.
Sienna came into The Grind. She was getting coffee on the way to class. Frankie was there. The ENERGY in that interaction.
They were polite. Too polite. That 'hi how are you' that means absolutely nothing. Sienna left quickly. Frankie was quiet the rest of his shift.
So my coworker and my volleyball teammate used to date. This is fine. Everything is fine. I'm just going to pretend I didn't notice.
The pigeon brought a friend again. Second pigeon era. They both stare now. Doubled surveillance.
Crawford rematch. This is the one we've been building toward. Hana's pregame speech was intense. Sienna was locked in. We were ready.
WE WON. Revenge is sweet. Three sets but we dominated the third. Sienna was MVP, her sets were untouchable. Crawford's captain looked devastated.
Hana actually lifted Sienna in the air. Reggie was screaming. I might have cried a little. Don't tell anyone.
Celebration at Theo's. Bigger than usual because this was a statement win. Even people who don't play volleyball showed up.
Sienna was in a great mood for the first time in weeks. She said winning against Crawford felt like 'closure on a lot of things.' Interesting word choice.
Zara and Andre were there being couple-y. Ivy was arguing with someone about rent control. Standard stuff.
Kwame snuck Mira into the party. They thought they were being discreet. They were not. I saw at least three people notice.
If February is the shortest month why does it feel the longest. Make it make sense.
Victor's dissertation defense is next week. He's been camping at The Grind reviewing notes. I've been giving him free chai lattes when Oscar's not looking.
Frankie finally talked about Sienna. Not by name but he mentioned his ex 'plays volleyball' and 'they run in similar circles now.' He seemed resigned more than upset.
He said they dated most of last year and it ended because 'they wanted different things.' Standard breakup stuff. He's over it but running into her is 'awkward.'
I just nodded and made sympathetic noises. Not going to mention I know exactly who his ex is. Some things are better left alone.
who all works at The Grind with me

It's still just the core four - Oscar running things, Frankie being Frankie, Juno and me holding it together. Oscar mentioned maybe hiring someone else for spring rush but for now we're a tight operation.
Spring break is in two weeks. Cameron confirmed he's visiting. Finally get to show him campus and introduce him to the disaster that is my life here.
Beatrice is already planning post-break study schedules. The woman does not rest. I admire and fear her.
Stats midterm approaching and I'm actually understanding things thanks to Priya's notes. She offered to study together. Said yes.
Studied with Priya at the library. She's really good at explaining concepts. Data science brain. We got food after. She's cool.
Kwame asked if Priya was 'something.' I said no, just study partners. He raised his eyebrows. Roommate judgment is real.
February ending finally. March begins. Midterms. Spring break. The home stretch of the semester.
Victor passed his dissertation defense!! He came into The Grind looking like a man reborn. We gave him his chai for free and he almost cried.
Oscar made a 'congratulations Dr. Victor' sign. It was very wholesome. The Grind is a community more than a coffee shop honestly.
Mira and Kwame are officially telling people. She's getting transferred to a different floor next semester so there's no conflict of interest. They planned this.
Tobias found out and won't stop making jokes about Kwame 'dating the enemy.' Mira took it in stride. She's cooler than I gave her credit for.
Why is 'queue' just the letter Q with four silent letters. English is a scam.
Stats midterm tomorrow. Priya and I crammed for three hours. I actually feel prepared? Suspicious.
Stats midterm done. Felt decent. Priya said she 'crushed it' which is probably accurate for her.
Volleyball game against Morrison. Easy win. We're looking strong for playoffs. Hana's already scouting future opponents.
Sienna seems fully back to her normal self. The weird Frankie tension has settled into 'we ignore each other's existence.' Healthy coping.
Cameron arrives tomorrow! Cleaned my half of the room which Kwame found hilarious. 'You have a floor,' he observed.
CAMERON IS HERE. He looks the same but also different? More confident maybe. Or just better rested than college students.
Campus tour went well. He was impressed by the science building even though it's far from everything. 'This is so college,' he kept saying.
Introduced him to Kwame who immediately started showing him photography stuff. They bonded over some video game I've never heard of.
Took Cameron to The Grind. Oscar was friendly, Frankie made him a fancy latte, Juno thought he was cute. Successful visit.
who's Cameron again
Best friend from high school. Known him forever. He goes to state school and is apparently surviving calculus now. Growth.
Party at Theo's with Cameron. He fit right in. Talked to everyone. At one point he and Ivy were debating something loudly while Zara watched.
Cameron said my college friends are 'actually cool, not just weird' which is high praise from him.
Took Cameron to see the volleyball team practice. Hana was in full captain mode. He was impressed by the 'intensity of intramural sports.'
Sienna talked to Cameron for a bit after practice. She was friendly, normal. Frankie situation clearly not affecting her ability to socialize.
Cameron left this morning. Weird to see him go. But also nice to have my space back. Balance.
He texted saying he wants to transfer here. Probably a joke? But maybe not? Would be wild to have him on campus.
Spring break technically starts this weekend but I'm staying on campus to work. Money over vacation.
Campus is emptying out. The quiet is nice but also eerie. Like the start of a horror movie.
Working extra shifts during break. Oscar's running a skeleton crew. Just me and Frankie mostly.
Frankie and I talked more without Juno or Oscar around. He's actually pretty deep when he's not in work mode. Philosophy minor apparently.
He mentioned his parents are in the restaurant industry which is why he's good with customers. 'Hospitality is in the blood,' he said.
Why do we drive on parkways and park on driveways. Wait I've thought about this before. My brain is recycling existential questions.
Kwame stayed for break too. Mira's here so that tracks. They've been spending a lot of time together.
The three of us got dinner together - me, Kwame, Mira. Like actual friends. The RA dynamic is completely gone now.
Mira's actually funny when she's not stressed about floor duties. She does impressions. Of professors. Kwame was dying laughing.
what's Mira's major
She told me over dinner - public health, wants to do healthcare administration. 'Making systems better from the inside,' she said. Explains the RA energy honestly. She likes fixing things.
Empty campus is peaceful but also makes you realize how much you need other people around. Humans are social creatures I guess.
Called mom for St. Patrick's Day even though we don't really celebrate. She made corned beef anyway. Traditions are weird.
Grandma Lucia called to complain about St. Patrick's Day. 'Too much green, not enough substance.' She's not wrong.
People starting to return. The noise is back. Strangely comforting.
Beatrice came back with new color-coded materials. She spent break 'organizing for success.' I spent break making lattes. Different approaches.
Study group reunion. Everyone looks rested except Dmitri who 'did research all break.' That man doesn't know how to relax.
Volleyball back in session. Playoffs start in April. Hana's been sending motivational videos to the group chat. Girl is committed.
what's our volleyball team record
I think we're like 6-1? Lost one early then won everything since. Strong contenders for the championship.
April approaches. The final stretch. Exams, playoffs, the end. Why does time move so weird.
APRIL FOOLS but I'm too tired to prank anyone. Nico tried to convince Beatrice the exam was moved up. She did not find it funny.
Oscar put salt in the sugar container as a joke. Three customers complained before we caught it. He felt terrible and gave out free drinks.
Zara invited me to some sociology department thing again. Andre's presenting this time. Supporting the boyfriend.
Andre's presentation was actually interesting. Something about social media and political polarization. Ivy had questions obviously.
Zara's friend group has expanded. There was a new girl there, Dani, who's apparently Andre's classmate. Quiet but seemed nice.
Orgo exam coming up. The big one before the final. Beatrice's study sessions have reached peak intensity.
Ronan held extra office hours and I stayed the whole time. His explanations are a gift. That man deserves tenure.
Playoff bracket came out. We play Harrison first round, then winner of Jackson/Mitchell. If we win both it's finals.
Hana made a whole strategy document. Like with diagrams. For intramural volleyball. This woman could run a war campaign.
If nothing sticks to Teflon how do they get Teflon to stick to the pan. These questions matter.
Mira and Kwame had their first public hangout that everyone saw. Holding hands in the dining hall. Reactions ranged from 'cute' to 'wait what.'
Tobias is milking the 'I knew first' narrative even though he absolutely did not know first. I did. But I'm not petty enough to correct him.
Celeste thinks they're cute together. 'Opposites attract,' she said. Kwame's chaos energy vs Mira's order energy. Makes sense actually.
First playoff game. Beat Harrison easily. Sienna was on fire. Reggie blocked everything. We're dialed in.
Semi-finals next weekend. Jackson beat Mitchell so that's our opponent. They're good but beatable.
Orgo exam tomorrow. Couldn't sleep. Same as always. Molecules in my dreams. This is fine.
Exam done. Felt harder than the last one but manageable. Beatrice looked confident. Nico looked like he'd seen a ghost.
Post-exam food at Sunrise Diner because tradition. Dmitri ordered coffee instead of food. 'Caffeine is all I need,' he said. Concerning.
Margaret asked if I was 'surviving the semester' and I said 'barely' and she laughed like I was joking. I was not joking.
Juno's been more confident at work lately. She handled a difficult customer without spiraling. Growth is real.
Oscar mentioned The Grind is doing better than the new competition across the street. Loyal customers won. He seemed relieved.
Semi-finals tomorrow. Nervous but ready. Hana's been texting 'WE GOT THIS' every few hours.
SEMI-FINALS WON. Jackson put up a fight but we closed it in three sets. Finals next week.
Crawford again. Of course it's Crawford. Rematch of the rematch. They beat whoever they played. This is destiny.
Team dinner at Sunrise Diner. Hana made us all share what we're grateful for. Corny but effective. This team is family now.
Sienna said she's grateful the season gave her 'something good to focus on when everything else was messy.' Her eyes got watery. Nobody pushed.
Orgo exam results: B+! BACK TO B+. Huang wrote 'better' which is basically a love letter from her.
Beatrice: A. Dmitri: A. Nico: B. The prophecy holds.
Stats exam grade came back too. B+. Priya got an A obviously. We celebrated with mediocre campus food.
who's on the volleyball team again

Mental roster check: Hana's captain obviously, Sienna sets, Reggie dominates the middle, I do my thing, plus the others who deserve more credit than I give them. Coach Tran keeps us honest. We're solid.
Finals week for volleyball AND approaching finals week for classes. The timing is poetic. Poetically terrible.
Practice was focused and calm. No screaming, no extra drills. Just running plays. Coach Tran gave a speech about enjoying the moment. He's really grown on me.
Championship game is Saturday. Three days. Deep breaths.
Why is it called rush hour when traffic barely moves. I've asked this before. The answer is still 'language is a lie.'
Kwame made a good luck card from Mira and him. Cute couple stuff. I'm happy for them even if it makes me feel single.
Zara texted that she and Andre and Ivy are coming to watch the championship. My college worlds are colliding.
Frankie said good luck when I mentioned the game. First time he's acknowledged volleyball even exists. Progress? Or just being polite.
Oscar gave me the day off Saturday without me asking. 'Go win something,' he said. Good boss.
CHAMPIONSHIP DAY. I'm nervous. Hana's nervous. Everyone's nervous but pretending not to be.
WE WON THE CHAMPIONSHIP. FIVE SETS. IT WENT TO FIVE SETS AND WE WON. I AM SCREAMING.
Sienna's final set in the fifth was PERFECT. Hana spiked it for the win. Reggie blocked their last attempt. TEAM EFFORT.
